2606.19469.title: Measuring Curriculum Alignment across Topical Coverage, Competency, and Cognitive Depth: A Longitudinal Framework Applied to CS2013 and CS2023
2606.19469.paper_summary: Undergraduate computer science is governed by international curricular guidelines revised about once a decade, yet programs lack a reliable, reproducible way to measure how completely they cover the current guidelines and how that coverage shifts when the guidelines are restructured. We address this with a human-in-the-loop pipeline that measures a program's coverage of an external body of knowledge, applied longitudinally to one accredited BSc in Computer Science against Computer Science Curricula 2013 (CS2013) and 2023 (CS2023). The pipeline represents the program and each guideline as structured corpora, generates candidate course-to-knowledge-unit matches by semantic retrieval, and confirms them through human judgment under an explicit coverage definition. Of seven benchmarked retrievers, a reciprocal-rank-fusion ensemble was strongest, and a reputed long-context model underperformed a small sentence model, so retriever choice must be measured. Both maps were validated by an independent second rater (Cohen's kappa 0.64 for CS2023, 0.69 for CS2013). The program covers 49.7% of CS2023 and 50.9% of CS2013 knowledge units, near-constant across a decade. Extending the same retrieve-then-confirm design to competency articulation and cognitive depth shows that the program articulates the competency for ~88% of covered units under each guideline, yet delivers it at the recommended depth for 76% of present units under CS2023 against 95% under CS2013, a gap reflecting the newer guideline's raised expectations, not the program. The longitudinal comparison separates persistent structural gaps (parallel and distributed computing, foundations of programming languages, systems fundamentals), uncovered against both guidelines and ABET, from differences that reflect the standard's evolution. The instrument is reusable and available from the authors on request.
2606.19469.paper_summary_zh: 本科計算機科學受到每十年左右修訂一次的國際課程指導方針的約束，但各個課程缺乏可靠且可重複的方法來衡量它們在多大程度上涵蓋了當前的指導方針，以及當指導方針重組時，這種涵蓋範圍如何變化。我們通過一個人機協作的流程來解決這個問題，該流程測量一個課程對外部知識體系的涵蓋程度，並將其應用於一個經認證的計算機科學學士學位課程，與2013年計算機科學課程（CS2013）和2023年計算機科學課程（CS2023）進行比較。該流程將課程和每個指導方針表示為結構化語料，通過語義檢索生成候選課程與知識單元的匹配，並在明確的涵蓋定義下通過人工判斷進行確認。在七個基準檢索器中，互惠排名融合集成模型最為強大，而一個知名的長上下文模型的表現不如小句子模型，因此檢索器的選擇必須進行測量。兩個映射均由獨立的第二評審者進行驗證（CS2023的Cohen's kappa為0.64，CS2013為0.69）。該課程涵蓋了49.7%的CS2023和50.9%的CS2013知識單元，這一比例在十年間幾乎保持不變。將相同的檢索-確認設計擴展到能力闡述和認知深度顯示，該課程在每個指導方針下對約88%的涵蓋單元進行了能力闡述，但在CS2023下以建議的深度提供的單元比例為76%，而在CS2013下為95%，這一差距反映了新指導方針提高的期望，而非課程本身的問題。縱向比較將持續的結構性差距（平行和分佈式計算、編程語言基礎、系統基礎）與反映標準演變的差異區分開來，這些差距在兩個指導方針和ABET下均未被發現。該工具是可重用的，並可根據要求向作者索取。
2606.19469.author: Sherzod Turaev et.al.
2606.19469.authors: Sherzod Turaev, Mary John, Saja Aldabet, Mamoun Awad, Nazar Zaki, Khaled Shuaib
